ddls:
CREATE TABLE "passengers" (
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each passenger */,
  "survived" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the passenger survived (1) or not (0) */,
  "pclass" INTEGER /* Passenger class (1st, 2nd, or 3rd) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Gender of the passenger */,
  "sibsp" INTEGER /* Number of siblings/spouses aboard the Titanic */,
  "parch" INTEGER /* Number of parents/children aboard the Titanic */,
  "fare" REAL /* Fare paid by the passenger */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class description (e.g., First, Second, Third) */,
  "who" TEXT /* Who the passenger is (e.g., man, woman, child) */,
  "adult_male" INTEGER /* Indicates if the passenger is an adult male */,
  "alive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the passenger is alive (yes or no) */,
  "alone" INTEGER /* Indicates if the passenger was alone */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the passenger */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the passenger (e.g., Mr., Ms., Mrs., Dr.) */,
  "cabin" TEXT /* Cabin number of the passenger */,
  "deck" TEXT /* Deck number of the passenger */,
  "embarked" TEXT /* Port of embarkation (e.g., Southampton, Cherbourg, Queenstown) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("passenger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survival_stats" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survival statistic record */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class category of the passengers */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the passengers (e.g., 0-10, 11-20, etc.) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the passengers */,
  "total_passengers" INTEGER /* Total number of passengers in this category */,
  "total_survived" INTEGER /* Total number of survivors in this category */,
  "survival_rate" REAL /* Calculated survival rate for this category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survival_stats_class FOREIGN KEY ("class") REFERENCES passenger_classes ("class_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "passenger_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each passenger class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the passenger class (e.g., First, Second, Third) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the class features */,
  "fare_range" TEXT /* Range of fares for this class (e.g., $0-$100, $100-$500, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship record */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the base passenger */,
  "related_passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related passenger */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., sibling, parent, spouse) */,
  "relation_degree" TEXT /* Degree of relationship (e.g., first-degree, second-degree, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_relationships_related_passenger_id FOREIGN KEY ("related_passenger_id") REFERENCES passengers ("passenger_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_relationships_passenger_id FOREIGN KEY ("passenger_id") REFERENCES passengers ("passenger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_tools" (
  "tool_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytical tool */,
  "tool_name" TEXT /* Name of the analytical tool */,
  "tool_description" TEXT /* Description of the analytical tool */,
  "tool_type" TEXT /* Type of the analytical tool (e.g., statistical, machine learning, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tool_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., research, marketing, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed by the user (e.g., dataset, tool, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., CSV, API, etc.) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_imports" (
  "import_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data import */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "import_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the import */,
  "import_status" TEXT /* Status of the import (e.g., success, failure, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("import_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)
